title: OSI, TCP/IP, Encapsulation, and Traffic Evidence · Protocol Recall
summary: Trace a client request through host, switch, router, firewall, service, and return path using captures and diagrams. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a layered packet-flow diagnosis after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain the responsibilities and failure evidence of all seven OSI reference-model layers.
Map TCP/IP link, internet, transport, and application functions to OSI layers without treating either as packet reality.
Trace data, segments or datagrams, packets, frames, and bits through encapsulation, forwarding, and decapsulation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Use a changed review case for network models flows that tests this competency: Interpret frame, packet, transport, and application capture fields while accounting for vantage point, encryption, offload, and missing traffic.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
